task_completion.description: Document the acute appendicitis diagnosis for patient Luna.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Conditions', 'Diseases', or 'Pathologies' section in Luna's patient record showing 'Acute appendicitis' or the ICD-10 code 'K35'.

task_completion.description: Record a clinical evaluation noting fever and tachycardia.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Evaluations', 'Encounters', or clinical notes screen for Luna that explicitly mentions 'fever' and 'tachycardia' in the signs/symptoms or clinical assessment fields.

task_completion.description: Order the standard pre-operative laboratory panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Lab Tests', 'Requests', or 'Orders' screen showing at least 3 distinct laboratory tests ordered for patient Luna.
Look for a 'Prescriptions' or 'Medications' screen for Luna showing an order for an appropriate antibiotic (e.g., ceftriaxone, metronidazole, cefazolin, or amoxicillin/clavulanate).

task_completion.description: Schedule a post-discharge surgical follow-up appointment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Appointments' or calendar screen showing a scheduled follow-up appointment for Luna, with a date set roughly 7 to 14 days in the future.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client being used to navigate and enter data, rather than a terminal window or API script.

integrity.description: Data was entered into the appropriate structured fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Information like diagnoses, lab orders, and prescriptions should be entered into their respective dedicated modules/forms in GNU Health, not just dumped as plain text into a single generic notepad or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows visible executing SQL commands (like psql or sqlite3) to directly insert records into the GNU Health database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is Luna (no last name). The expected ICD-10 diagnosis code for acute appendicitis is K35. The pre-operative lab panel must contain at least 3 lab orders. Acceptable antibiotics include those matching ceftriaxone, metronidazole, piperacillin, tazobactam, cefazolin, or amoxicillin/clavulanate. The post-discharge follow-up appointment should be scheduled roughly 7 to 14 days after the task start date.
